_comment: Golden vectors for the CANONICAL Locator serialization (Locator.canonicalJSON in vreader/Models/Locator.swift) — vreader's engine-neutral cross-device-resume contract. Sorted keys, nil omission, bookFingerprint inlined with prefix, floats '%.6f' (POSIX), NFC normalization of string fields, \r\n/\r normalized to \n, RFC-8259 escaping. Both the Swift conformance test (the reference) and the Kotlin reference impl must produce the EXACT expectedCanonicalJSON string -> identical canonicalHash -> cross-platform position identity. cfi is intentionally absent from the canonical-resume vectors (classified platform-local->lossy-fallback in identity/locator.md).

vectors._why: EPUB position — href + progression + totalProgression are the canonical cross-device resume anchors.
TXT position — UTF-16 char offset + text quote anchor (CJK; precomposed, NFC-stable).
PDF position — page index is document-defined, canonical across renderers (PDFKit <-> PdfiumAndroid).

Canonicalization-rule coverage (Codex Gate-4): RFC-8259 escaping (quote/backslash), CR-LF + bare-CR normalization to LF, PAIRED char range (charRangeEnd sorts before charRangeStart). textQuote input 'a"b\c' -> 'a"b\c'; textContextBefore 'p
qr' -> 'p
q
r'.

NFC normalization (feature #109/bug #356): href input NFD 'a\u0301.html' + textQuote NFD 'cafe\u0301' (base + U+0301) MUST canonicalize to the NFC precomposed form on BOTH Swift and Kotlin. iOS now NFC-normalizes (V9->V10 migration re-keys existing rows).
Numeric boundaries: progression 0.0 / totalProgression 1.0 / large charOffset (Int max) — float '%.6f' edges + large int formatting must agree.
Empty/minimal: only the bookFingerprint triple, no optional fields — the all-nil baseline.
